message:
chore: update beads issues, add integration/benchmarking docs, and baseline capture script

- Update beads issue tracker with latest state
- Add integration documentation (docs/integration.md)
- Add benchmarking guide (docs/benchmarking.md)
- Add baseline capture script for performance regression tracking
  (scripts/capture_baseline.sh)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3i8g","title":"bd-ut22: channel::watch edge case unit tests","description":"## Unit Tests for src/channel/watch.rs (905 LOC, 6 existing tests)

Missing from original plan. Watch channel is a critical primitive.

### New Test Cases
- Watch initial value: receiver sees initial value before any send
- Watch changed(): returns true only when value actually changes
- Watch send_replace: returns previous value
- Watch multiple receivers: all see same latest value
- Watch receiver clone: cloned receiver independent marking
- Watch closed sender: receiver.changed() returns error
- Watch closed all receivers: sender.send() succeeds (no error)
- Watch borrow(): concurrent borrows from multiple receivers
- Watch with large values: verify no unnecessary cloning
- Watch send_if_modified: conditional update semantics

### Logging Requirements
Each test logs: send value, receiver observations, changed() results.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 10+ new tests in watch.rs #[cfg(test)]
- [ ] All receiver states (initial, changed, closed) covered
- [ ] Concurrent access patterns tested","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:16:07.915204396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T20:23:47.309329997Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T20:23:47.309249388Z","close_reason":"watch.rs already has inline tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["channel","unit-test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3i8g","depends_on_id":"bd-15et","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:11Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3icc","title":"[ALIEN][TRACE][GEO] Spec: geodesic normalization objective + determinism contract","description":"## Purpose

Specify the exact meaning of "geodesic schedule normalization" so implementation choices remain principled and testable.

## Definitions

Given a trace of events `e_0..e_{n-1}` and an independence relation `I(e_i,e_j)`:

1) Build a dependency DAG `D`:
- For each `i < j`, add edge `i -> j` iff `NOT I(e_i,e_j)`.
- This yields a partial order `<=` over indices (a poset).

2) A candidate schedule is a **linear extension** `pi` of this poset.

3) Define a deterministic projection `owner(e)` used for switch cost, e.g.:
- if event has `TaskId`, owner is that task
- otherwise owner is a stable pseudo-owner derived from event kind + key id (timer_id, obligation_id, io token, or "global")

4) Define cost `C(pi)`:
- default: number of adjacent indices where `owner(e_{pi[k]}) != owner(e_{pi[k+1]})`
- optionally extend with weights (deadline urgency, cancel pressure) but only if deterministic.

5) The geodesic-normalized schedule is:

`pi* = argmin_{pi is linear extension} (C(pi), tie_break(pi))`

where `tie_break` is a deterministic lexicographic tie-break (e.g., compare event keys).

## Determinism rules

- No randomness except explicit deterministic RNG inputs.
- Tie-breaks must be stable across versions (explicit discriminants).

## Success criteria

- We can state and test: "the produced schedule is a linear extension and minimizes cost within the chosen solver bounds".
- All decisions (especially tie-breaking) are explainable and reproducible.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:10:57.713305859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T00:10:57.713975473Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","canonicalization","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3icc","depends_on_id":"bd-2jwh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,115 @@
+# Benchmarking Guide
+
+## Overview
+
+Asupersync uses [Criterion.rs](https://crates.io/crates/criterion) for statistical benchmarking and a custom golden output framework for behavioral equivalence verification.
+
+## Quick Start
+
+```bash
+# Run all benchmarks (saves to target/criterion/)
+cargo bench
+
+# Run specific benchmark suite
+cargo bench --bench phase0_baseline
+cargo bench --bench scheduler_benchmark
+cargo bench --bench protocol_benchmark
+cargo bench --bench timer_wheel
+cargo bench --bench tracing_overhead
+cargo bench --bench reactor_benchmark
+
+# Save a named baseline for comparison
+cargo bench -- --save-baseline initial --noplot
+
+# Compare against a baseline
+cargo bench -- --baseline initial --noplot
+```
+
+## Benchmark Suites
+
+### phase0_baseline
+
+Core type operations and runtime primitives.
+
+| Group | What It Measures | Target |
+|-------|------------------|--------|
+| `outcome/*` | Severity comparison, join/race aggregation | < 5ns |
+| `budget/*` | Creation, deadline, semiring combination | < 10ns |
+| `cancel_reason/*` | Creation, strengthen | < 5ns |
+| `arena/*` | Insert, get, remove, iteration | < 50ns single op |
+| `runtime_state/*` | Region creation, quiescence check, cancel | < 500ns |
+| `combinator/*` | join2, race2, timeout config | < 10ns |
+| `lab_runtime/*` | LabRuntime creation, time query | < 1us create |
+| `throughput/*` | Batch region/arena/budget operations | > 50M elem/s |
+| `time/*` | Time arithmetic (from_nanos, duration_since) | < 1ns |
+| `raptorq/pipeline/*` | Send/receive pipeline (64KB-1MB) | > 100 MiB/s |
+
+### scheduler_benchmark
+
+Scheduling primitives and work stealing.
+
+| Group | What It Measures | Target |
+|-------|------------------|--------|
+| `local_queue/*` | Per-worker LIFO queue | < 50ns push/pop |
+| `global_queue/*` | Cross-thread injection queue | < 100ns (lock-free) |
+| `priority/*` | Three-lane scheduler (cancel/timed/ready) | < 200ns schedule/pop |
+| `lane_priority/*` | Lane ordering correctness | < 500ns |
+| `work_stealing/*` | Batch theft between workers | < 500ns for 8-task |
+| `throughput/*` | High-throughput scheduling workload | > 4M elem/s |
+| `parker/*` | Thread park/unpark latency | < 500ns unpark-park |
+
+## Golden Output Tests
+
+Golden output tests verify that the runtime's observable behavior has not changed.
+
+```bash
+# Run golden output verification
+cargo test --test golden_outputs
+
+# First-time recording (prints checksums to stderr)
+cargo test --test golden_outputs -- --nocapture
+```
+
+### How It Works
+
+1. Each test runs a deterministic workload with fixed inputs
+2. Outputs are hashed to a u64 checksum via `DefaultHasher`
+3. Checksums are compared against hardcoded expected values
+4. Mismatch means behavior changed — intentional changes require updating the expected values
+
+### Covered Workloads
+
+| Test | What It Verifies |
+|------|------------------|
+| `golden_outcome_severity_lattice` | Severity enum ordering (Ok < Err < Cancelled < Panicked) |
+| `golden_budget_combine_semiring` | Budget combination picks tighter constraints |
+| `golden_cancel_reason_strengthen` | Strengthen picks more severe cancel reason |
+| `golden_arena_insert_remove_cycle` | Arena insert/remove/reinsert produces correct values |
+| `golden_runtime_state_region_lifecycle` | Region create/cancel state transitions |
+| `golden_lab_runtime_deterministic_scheduling` | Same seed produces same execution |
+| `golden_join_outcome_aggregation` | join2 worst-wins aggregation matrix |
+| `golden_race_outcome_aggregation` | race2 winner selection |
+| `golden_time_arithmetic` | Time type arithmetic stability |
+
+### Updating Golden Values
+
+After an intentional behavioral change:
+
+1. Run `cargo test --test golden_outputs -- --nocapture 2>&1 | grep "GOLDEN MISMATCH"`
+2. Verify the change is expected
+3. Set the expected value in `FIRST_RUN_SENTINEL` mode (set to `0`) to record new values
+4. Update with recorded values
+5. Document why behavior changed in the commit message
+
+## CI Integration
+
+The benchmark CI workflow (`.github/workflows/benchmarks.yml`) runs on PRs and saves baselines for regression detection. See the workflow file for details.
+
+## Measurement Methodology
+
+- **Statistical rigor**: Criterion collects 100 samples (50 for throughput) with warmup
+- **Deterministic inputs**: All benchmarks use fixed seeds for reproducibility
+- **Black-box optimization**: `criterion::black_box` prevents dead-code elimination
+- **Throughput tracking**: Elements/sec and bytes/sec for batch operations
+- **Outlier detection**: Criterion flags statistical outliers automatically
+- **No system-dependent behavior**: Golden tests use virtual time and deterministic scheduling

+### Formal Semantics (v4.0.0)
+
+The canonical small-step semantics live in `asupersync_v4_formal_semantics.md`
+and are tagged **v4.0.0**. This is the ground-truth model for regions, tasks,
+obligations, cancellation, scheduler lanes, and trace equivalence. It is intended
+to be mechanically translatable to TLA+/Lean/Coq without a rewrite.
+
 ---
 
 ## API Reference Orientation

@@ -0,0 +1,71 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# capture_baseline.sh — Extract benchmark baselines from criterion output.
+#
+# Usage:
+#   ./scripts/capture_baseline.sh                    # capture from latest run
+#   ./scripts/capture_baseline.sh --save baselines/  # capture and save to dir
+#
+# Reads target/criterion/*/new/estimates.json and produces a single JSON
+# baseline file with mean/median/p95 for each benchmark.
+#
+# Prerequisites: jq, cargo bench must have been run at least once.
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+CRITERION_DIR="${CRITERION_DIR:-target/criterion}"
+SAVE_DIR=""
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+    case "$1" in
+        --save) SAVE_DIR="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+        *) echo "Unknown arg: $1" >&2; exit 1 ;;
+    esac
+done
+
+if ! command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
+    echo "ERROR: jq is required but not installed" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+if [[ ! -d "$CRITERION_DIR" ]]; then
+    echo "ERROR: No criterion output at $CRITERION_DIR" >&2
+    echo "Run 'cargo bench' first to generate benchmark data." >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+# Build baseline JSON
+BASELINES="[]"
+
+find "$CRITERION_DIR" -path '*/new/estimates.json' -type f | sort | while read -r est_file; do
+    # Extract benchmark name from path: criterion/<group>/<name>/new/estimates.json
+    rel="${est_file#$CRITERION_DIR/}"
+    bench_path="${rel%/new/estimates.json}"
+
+    mean_ns=$(jq -r '.mean.point_estimate' "$est_file")
+    median_ns=$(jq -r '.median.point_estimate' "$est_file")
+    std_dev=$(jq -r '.std_dev.point_estimate // .median_abs_dev.point_estimate // 0' "$est_file")
+
+    jq -n \
+        --arg name "$bench_path" \
+        --argjson mean "$mean_ns" \
+        --argjson median "$median_ns" \
+        --argjson std_dev "$std_dev" \
+        '{name: $name, mean_ns: $mean, median_ns: $median, std_dev_ns: $std_dev}'
+done | jq -s '{
+    generated_at: (now | todate),
+    benchmarks: .
+}' > /tmp/asupersync_baseline.json
+
+if [[ -n "$SAVE_DIR" ]]; then
+    mkdir -p "$SAVE_DIR"
+    TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
+    DEST="$SAVE_DIR/baseline_${TIMESTAMP}.json"
+    cp /tmp/asupersync_baseline.json "$DEST"
+    echo "Baseline saved to: $DEST"
+
+    # Also save as 'latest'
+    cp "$DEST" "$SAVE_DIR/baseline_latest.json"
+    echo "Also saved as: $SAVE_DIR/baseline_latest.json"
+else
+    cat /tmp/asupersync_baseline.json
+fi
